description: A loosely rendered impressionistic painting of an architectural niche or window in the Vatican. The composition features a grand opening framed by two large Corinthian columns supporting an entablature, with a balustrade at the bottom. Within the shadowed recess stands an equestrian statue — a figure on horseback — partially obscured by the deep contrast between the sunlit facade and the dark interior. The stonework is painted in warm ochre and cream tones with confident, fluid brushwork characteristic of Sargent's plein-air style.
